operational_knowledge_units.title: Tote presence and empty rack color indicators in Container Selection
operational_knowledge_units.summary: In the Container Selection tote view, dark blue indicates a tote is present and light blue indicates an empty rack.
observed_evidence: Slide text states: 'Dark blue indicates the presence of a tote. While light blue represents empty rack.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Container Selection Totes UI reference: dark blue means a tote is present; light blue means the rack is empty.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Selecting a tote location shows a box icon and tote number
operational_knowledge_units.summary: When a tote location is selected in the UI, a box icon appears along with the tote number.
observed_evidence: Slide text states: 'If you select it, a box icon will show as well as the tote number.'

relationships.to: Box icon and tote number displayed

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Container Selection tote behavior: selecting a tote location causes the UI to display a box icon and the tote number.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Tote lookup by number can identify associated rack or AGV
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The UI supports finding a tote by entering its number and selecting the rack or AGV where it is located, if the tote exists in the system.
observed_evidence: Slide text states: 'You can also find a tote by typing the number in and selecting the rack or AGV it’s located on if its in the system.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Container Selection tote lookup: enter a tote number to find the tote and identify the rack or AGV where it is located, if the tote is in the system.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Find a tote by tote number in Container Selection
preconditions: User is on the Container Selection tote interface.
The tote may need to exist in the system for location results to appear.
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide describes tote identification colors and states that a tote can be found by typing its number and selecting the rack or AGV where it is located.

steps.action: Enter the tote number in the tote search/input area.
steps.observed_evidence: Slide text: 'You can also find a tote by typing the number in...'
steps.visual_region_hint: Container Selection / Totes UI area with tote lookup field referenced by slide text

steps.action: Select the rack or AGV where the tote is located.
steps.observed_evidence: Slide text: '...and selecting the rack or AGV it’s located on...'
steps.visual_region_hint: Rack or AGV selection area in the Container Selection / Totes interface

warnings: The slide states lookup works only 'if its in the system.'
Exact field names and click targets are not visible on the slide.
procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Procedure candidate for Container Selection tote lookup: type the tote number, then select the rack or AGV where the tote is located. Result depends on the tote being present in the system.
